prediction.summary: An insider threat incident involving user 'emma.hr' has been detected. The employee, who submitted her resignation 48 hours prior, accessed multiple sensitive HR and legal documents, performed a bulk download of 842MB, copied the data to a removable USB device, and initiated a sync to a personal cloud storage service. These actions, including accessing files outside her normal role scope, triggered multiple security alerts indicating a high-severity data exfiltration attempt.

timeline.event: User emma.hr performed bulk download of 842MB archive /tmp/eh_export.zip
User emma.hr copied 842MB to removable device E:\backup
User emma.hr connected personal cloud sync client sync.evil-drop.io
DLP policy violation: HR confidential data staged for external sync by emma.hr
SIEM detected insider threat pattern: bulk sensitive file access + USB + cloud sync
SIEM noted user emma.hr's resignation submitted 48 hours ago, elevating risk score
SIEM identified MITRE T1530 Data from Cloud Storage / internal collection
SIEM generated HIGH severity alert for insider_threat by user emma.hr
HR audit confirmed emma.hr accessed 47 files outside normal role scope in 54 minutes

recommendations: Immediately disable 'emma.hr's accounts and revoke all active sessions to prevent further exfiltration.
Isolate 'LAPTOP-HR-09' for forensic imaging and analysis to determine the full scope of data copied and identify any other malicious artifacts.
Block access to 'sync.evil-drop.io' at the network perimeter to prevent further cloud synchronization.
Conduct a full audit of all files accessed by 'emma.hr' to identify all potentially compromised sensitive data.
Review and enhance offboarding procedures, including automated access revocation and stricter DLP enforcement for departing employees.
